hero.eyebrow: Open-source teaching platform · powered by a real school IS
hero.title: Don't teach about systems. Teach inside one.
hero.sub: EduStack IS is an open-source school information system designed for the computer-science classroom. Younger students explore real data and roles; older students dig into the code and run it themselves.

idea.title: A school IS is the best CS textbook you never wrote.
idea.body: A school information system is a microcosm of every topic on the computer-science curriculum: role-based access, relational data, scheduling, audit logs, integration with AI — applied to a domain students already know intimately. EduStack IS turns that into hands-on lessons your class can actually use, instead of toy examples that explain nothing.

pillars.sharedBody: Students work with the same data structures and constraints schools actually use — not contrived textbook scenarios.
pillars.tailoredBody: Lessons scale from 'click around the dashboard' for primary school to 'fork, build, deploy' for high school.
pillars.openBody: MIT-licensed. Czech-friendly. The whole stack is documented and version-controlled alongside the methodology.

how.title: What a lesson on EduStack looks like.

steps.title: Pick a role
steps.body: Admin, teacher, student, parent — sign in as one of them. Discover what they can see and do.

steps.title: Follow a real workflow
steps.body: Import a class, enter grades, plan a substitution. The screens and rules are the ones a real school would use.

steps.title: Read the constraints
steps.body: How does the system decide who can do what? Open the role matrix and trace it back to the data model.

steps.title: Break something on purpose
steps.body: Try an action the role isn't allowed. Watch the audit log. Learn what the system protects and why.

steps.title: Compare with your own school
steps.body: What's the same? What's different? What does your school's spreadsheet workflow do that this one doesn't?

learn.title: One system, three age tiers.
learn.sub: Each tier ships with worksheets, lesson plans and teacher notes — written for Czech CS classrooms.

tiers.label: Primary
tiers.body: Click around as different roles. Notice which screens exist for whom. Match permissions to job titles. Discover that 'data' has shape.

tiers.label: Secondary
tiers.body: Import a CSV. Read an ER diagram. Spot a permission rule. Find an audit-log entry that proves who did what.

tiers.label: High school
tiers.body: Clone the repo. Run it locally. Read the API code. Hack on a feature. Open a pull request against the real codebase.

tour.title: The demonstration tool, up close.
tour.sub: Real screenshots from the running app. Click any image to open it full-screen — or jump into the full user manual for context.

teachers.title: Less prep. More discussion.
bullets: Worksheets ready for primary, secondary and high school — with answer keys.
Methodology guides for every chapter: what to ask, what to point out.
No installation needed in class — the live demo is open all week.
Czech grading scale and Czech UI built in.
Reset the demo on Monday morning and start fresh.

students.title: School software, finally on your side.
bullets: See exactly what data your school holds about you.
Try every role — including the headmaster's chair.
The code is open. You're allowed to read it.
Run the whole thing on your laptop if you want.
Submit issues, send pull requests, leave your mark.

method.title: The methodology is the product.
method.body: EduStack IS is the demonstration. The methodology — worksheets, role matrices, ER diagrams, teacher notes — is what your class actually uses. Everything is documented and version-controlled alongside the code.

items.title: Worksheets per grade
items.body: Primary, secondary, high school — with answer keys.

items.title: ER diagrams
items.body: The schema is documented, not implied.

downloads.title: Lesson materials. Take them to class.
downloads.sub: Everything a CS teacher needs to run a lesson on EduStack — worksheets per age tier, role matrices, ER diagrams. PDF and Google Docs.

items.title: High school worksheets
items.body: High school (Informatics, Programming). Run it locally. Read the code. Build something.

items.title: Methodology master folder
items.body: The whole package, live. Also contains a database backups subfolder.

demo.title: Click around. Bring it to class.
demo.sub: A live testing instance with seed data for every role. Reset every Monday — bring a fresh class without fixtures going stale.

source.title: Read the code. Open an issue. Send a PR.

repos.name: edustack-thesis
repos.body: Final materials and the bachelor's thesis will be linked here after the defense.

contact.title: If you teach CS, let's talk.
contact.body: EduStack IS is a bachelor's thesis project, built by one person on purpose. If you'd like to pilot it in your school, adapt a worksheet, or just have feedback — drop a line.

Worksheets.title: Working Sheets
Worksheets.description: Detailed scenarios and methodological guides extracted from the bachelor's thesis.

primary.title: Primary School (4th-5th Grade)
primary.lesson1: Explorer and the Mystery of Roles
primary.lesson2: A Day in the Teacher's Role
primary.lesson3: The Director and the Whole School

Manual.title: User Manual
Manual.intro: Complete guide to setting up and operating the Edu Stack Information System.

tech_stack.title: Technology Stack
tech_stack.description: The Edu Stack IS is built on a modern, robust, and highly scalable cloud-native infrastructure.

nestjs.title: NestJS
nestjs.description: A progressive Node.js framework for building efficient, reliable and scalable server-side applications. It uses TypeScript and combines elements of OOP, FP, and FRP.

react.title: React
react.description: A JavaScript library for building component-based user interfaces. It enables efficient rendering and reactive state management.

cloudflare_d1.title: Fly.io + SQLite
cloudflare_d1.description: The backend and MCP server run together on a single Fly.io machine per environment, with the SQLite database stored on a persistent 1 GB volume mounted at /data. better-sqlite3 is used for direct file access.

mcp.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
mcp.description: An open protocol that enables AI models to have secure and standardized access to local or remote data and tools.

nextjs.title: Next.js
nextjs.description: The React framework for the web, enabling server-side rendering and static site generation for optimal performance and SEO.

tailwind.title: Tailwind CSS
tailwind.description: A utility-first CSS framework for rapidly building modern websites without ever leaving your HTML.

modules.title: Application Modules
modules.description: The system is designed as a modular platform where each component serves a specific pedagogical or administrative purpose.

admin.title: System Administration
admin.built: A set of core services for multi-tenant school lifecycle management, global configuration, and security auditing.
admin.benefit: Enables global platform orchestration, secure tenant isolation, and centralized resource management.

auth.title: Auth & SSO
auth.built: Secure authentication layer with support for traditional credentials and enterprise SSO (Google, GitHub, Microsoft).
auth.benefit: Ensures data integrity and robust Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) across the entire platform.

registry.title: School Registry
registry.built: Core database module for managing students, staff, classes, and physical building structures.
registry.benefit: Serves as the central 'Source of Truth' for all other educational and administrative modules.

schedule.title: Interactive Scheduling
schedule.built: Sophisticated engine for timetable creation with real-time conflict detection and substitution management.
schedule.benefit: Automates the most complex school administrative tasks and eliminates scheduling human errors.

grading.title: Grading & Evaluation
grading.built: Comprehensive system for tracking marks, weighted averages, and complex verbal evaluations.
grading.benefit: Provides immediate academic transparency for teachers, students, and parents.

ai.title: Pedagogical AI Assistant
ai.built: Deep integration of LLMs into teacher workflows for lesson planning, material generation, and report polishing.
ai.benefit: Significantly reduces administrative burden on teachers while bringing modern AI tools to the classroom.

mcp.title: MCP API (AI Integration)
mcp.built: A standardized bridge that connects external AI agents with live system data through a secure tool-based interface.
mcp.benefit: Allows students and developers to experiment with building autonomous AI agents on top of school data.

data_model.title: Data Model
data_model.description: Entity-relationship diagrams derived directly from the production database schema. The schema is split into eight bounded contexts so each diagram fits on one screen. Click any thumbnail to open the full-resolution image.
data_model.caption: Generated from apps/backend/src/database/schema.sql via Mermaid.
data_model.fullDescription: All ~40 tables, attributes and relationships on a single canvas. Scroll inside the frame to pan, or click to open the full-resolution image in a new tab.

tenancy.title: Tenancy & Identity
tenancy.description: Multi-school tenancy, users, roles, parent-student links, SSO identities, and audit log.

academic.title: Academic Structure & Curriculum
academic.description: Academic years, semesters, grade levels, student enrollment, curriculum versions, subject templates and instances, RVP competencies.

workload.title: Workload & Physical Space
workload.description: Teacher and staff workload, subject assignments, buildings, rooms and room sharing between schools.

schedule.title: Schedule & Class Book
schedule.description: Lesson timetable, substitutions, recurring events, snapshots, and the digital class book with teacher signatures.

grading.title: Attendance, Absences & Grading
grading.description: Attendance records, parent absence excuses, grades, report cards, behavior and competency grades, educational measures, commission exams and classification deadlines.

planning.title: Planning & Teaching Materials
planning.description: Thematic plans split by week, lesson preparations and uploaded teaching materials linked to subjects and grade levels.

communication.title: Communication & Community
communication.description: Conversations, messages and attachments, notifications, bulletin posts, polls with options and votes, calendar events with RSVPs, and school-wide events.

system.title: System, AI & Misc
system.description: System settings, API key secrets, AI token usage, global config, and ad-hoc user tasks.

competency_matrix.title: Role Permission Matrix
competency_matrix.description: Authoritative mapping of which user role can perform which operation, sourced from the @Roles decorators in the backend controllers. Operations are grouped by the same eight domains as the data model.

system_architecture.title: System Architecture
system_architecture.description: Application components in ArchiMate notation and a user-perspective view showing which screens and services each role touches.
system_architecture.componentsDescription: Backend modules grouped by domain (Core platform, Education, Communication, Intelligence). The backend + MCP server run on a Fly.io machine with SQLite on a persistent volume; the frontend is built and served from Cloudflare Pages. Cloudflare R2 is used for off-machine backups. REST + MCP are the two public service surfaces; external systems (LLM providers, SSO IdPs, SMTP, MCP clients) are shown separately. The diagram still shows the previous Cloudflare-only layout — see the Deployment section below for the current topology.
system_architecture.actorsDescription: Seven user roles plus an external MCP/API developer, mapped to the screens or portals they use and the backend services that serve them.

processes.title: Key processes
processes.description: Sequence diagrams for representative flows across the system — administration, authentication, AI orchestration, and day-to-day school operations.

init-setup.title: Initial system setup
init-setup.description: First-run wizard: create the first system admin from scratch, or restore the system from a SQLite backup.

login.title: Login (credentials + SSO)
login.description: Local credential authentication (Argon2 + JWT cookie) and SSO via Google / GitHub / Microsoft with email-match policy.

generate-data.title: Generate test data
generate-data.description: System admin seeds an entire school — classes, users, schedule, grades, attendance — optionally using AI for school and student names.

backup-restore.title: Backup and restore
backup-restore.description: Three flows in one diagram: the nightly cron backup, an ad-hoc admin backup, and restoring the database from R2.

ai-generation.title: AI-assisted generation
ai-generation.description: Teacher requests a thematic plan or report-card polish; backend decrypts the provider key, calls the LLM, and logs token usage.

substitution.title: Schedule substitution
substitution.description: Deputy assigns a substitute teacher/room; collision detector validates against existing schedule before persisting and notifying affected users.

absence-excuse.title: Absence excuse
absence-excuse.description: Parent submits an excuse; homeroom teacher reviews and either approves (attendance reconciled) or rejects with a reason.

development.title: Local Development
development.description: End-to-end setup of the monorepo on your machine. The frontend has no custom .env — it uses Vite's proxy to forward /api to the backend. All configuration lives in a single .env file at the repo root, consumed by both the NestJS backend and the MCP server.

node.label: Node.js 20+
node.description: Runtime for backend, MCP server and the build chain.

npm.label: npm 10+
npm.description: Bundled with Node — used to install monorepo workspaces.

flyctl.label: flyctl (optional)
flyctl.description: Fly.io CLI. Only needed to deploy your own environment. Install: brew install flyctl or curl -L https://fly.io/install.sh | sh.

secrets.title: Generate the two required secrets
secrets.command:
openssl rand -base64 64  # JWT_SECRET
openssl rand -base64 32  # ENCRYPTION_KEY

dev.title: Start backend, frontend and MCP server

development.urlsTitle: Default URLs after npm run dev
development.frontendNote: The React/Vite app has no .env file. In dev, Vite proxies every /api request to http://127.0.0.1:3000 (see apps/frontend/vite.config.ts), so the only "config" it needs is the backend running on PORT 3000. In production builds the API base URL is read from VITE_API_URL (set by the deploy workflow to https://be-sandbox-N.is-edustack.org) and baked into the bundle Cloudflare Pages serves.

env.title: .env reference
env.description: Every variable consumed by the backend or MCP server, grouped by concern. Examples in the table are the .env.example defaults; values shown as a shell command should be replaced with the command's output.

vars.jwt-secret: Symmetric key used to sign and verify the JWT cookies issued at login. Rotating it logs everyone out. MUST be unique per deployment.
vars.encryption-key: AES-256 key used to encrypt sensitive columns (provider API keys in SystemSecret, etc.). Losing it makes encrypted rows unreadable.
vars.settings-encryption-key: Optional separate key used by the legacy SystemSettings table. Falls back to ENCRYPTION_KEY when empty.
vars.enable-login-helper: When true, the login screen shows a panel with one-click demo users. Always set to false in production.
vars.login-helper-roles: Comma-separated allow-list of roles the helper surfaces. Lets you hide STUDENT/PARENT in a teacher-only demo, for example.
vars.demo-password: Password every seeded demo user is given. Used by the seeder; only relevant when AUTO_SEED is on.
vars.db-adapter: Selects the database driver. Keep "sqlite" — better-sqlite3 reads a local file in dev and the Fly volume at /data/edustack.db in production.
vars.database-url: Path to the local SQLite file. Used by Prisma CLI tools (introspection, migrations); the runtime opens the file directly via better-sqlite3.
vars.smtp-host: Hostname of the SMTP server. In local dev this is the bundled MailDev (npm run dev starts it on 1025).
vars.smtp-port: Port of the SMTP server. 1025 for MailDev, 587/465 for production providers.
vars.smtp-user: SMTP username. Empty for MailDev; required for SendGrid / SES.
vars.smtp-pass: SMTP password / API key. Always pass via secret manager in production.
vars.mail-from: From: address used on every transactional email (invites, password resets, notifications).
vars.port: Port the NestJS backend listens on.
vars.mcp-port: Port the MCP server listens on.
vars.cors-origin: Allowed origin for the REST API. Set to the frontend URL.
vars.frontend-url: Public URL of the frontend. Used to build links inside emails (invites, password reset).
vars.mcp-server-url: Where the backend looks for the MCP server. The /sse suffix is the MCP transport endpoint.
vars.mcp-host: Bind address for the MCP server. Default 127.0.0.1 (loopback) means it's unreachable from the network — set to 0.0.0.0 only when fronted by a reverse proxy AND when MCP_AUTH_TOKEN is set.
vars.mcp-auth-token: Shared bearer token. When set, /sse, /message and /v1/chat/completions require Authorization: Bearer '<token>'. The backend forwards it automatically.
vars.mcp-cors-origin: Comma-separated CORS allow-list. Leave empty to disable CORS (correct for loopback-only deployments).
vars.opencode-base-url: Base URL of the OpenAI-compatible OpenCode provider. Defaults to the local MCP server; in production MUST be https://.
vars.opencode-api-key: Bearer token for the OpenCode provider (when fronted by an authenticated MCP).
vars.google-ai-api-key: Google Gemini API key. Either GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY is accepted.
vars.gemini-api-key: Alias of GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY for historical reasons.
vars.openai-api-key: OpenAI API key. Used when the OpenAI provider is selected in system settings.
vars.anthropic-api-key: Anthropic API key. Used when the Anthropic provider is selected.
vars.r2-endpoint: Cloudflare R2 S3-compatible endpoint URL. Leave empty to fall back to local file backups in BACKUP_DIR.
vars.r2-access-key-id: R2 access key. Treat as a secret (in production set via flyctl secrets set R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=… --app edustack-sandbox-N).
vars.r2-secret-access-key: R2 secret key. Treat as a secret.
vars.r2-bucket-name: Name of the R2 bucket to write backups to.
vars.backup-dir: Filesystem path used when R2_* variables are empty. Created on first backup.
vars.auto-backup: If true, the BackupScheduler creates a nightly backup automatically.
vars.node-env: Standard Node environment. Set to "production" outside of local dev.
vars.auto-seed: If true, the seeder runs on the first start of a fresh database. Creates the demo school and the default System Admin.
vars.seed-file: Path to an alternative seed JSON. Empty uses the bundled default scenario.
vars.seed-admin-email: Email of the auto-created System Admin.
vars.seed-admin-first-name: First name of the auto-created System Admin.
vars.seed-admin-last-name: Last name of the auto-created System Admin.
vars.seed-admin-password: Initial password for the auto-created System Admin.

deployment.title: Deployment
deployment.description: Sandboxed, per-environment deployments. Each env_id provisions its own Fly.io machine (backend + MCP) with a 1 GB persistent SQLite volume, a sibling Fly.io app running MailDev as the per-env mailbox, an automatically created Cloudflare R2 bucket for database backups, and its own Cloudflare Pages project (frontend) — all wired to is-edustack.org subdomains.

backend.title: Backend + MCP server
backend.description: One Fly.io app per environment (edustack-sandbox-N) runs both Node processes in a single container. A 1 GB volume mounted at /data persists the SQLite database. The MCP server listens on 127.0.0.1:3001 inside the container — only the backend can reach it.

frontend.title: Frontend
frontend.description: One Cloudflare Pages project per environment (edustack-frontend-sandbox-N), built from apps/frontend with VITE_API_URL pointing at the matching backend.

maildev.title: MailDev mailbox
maildev.description: A second Fly.io app per environment (mail-edustack-sandbox-N) runs MailDev. The backend connects to its SMTP on the private 6PN network (mail-edustack-sandbox-N.internal:1025); the web inbox is exposed publicly at mail-sandbox-N.is-edustack.org for demos.

backups.title: R2 backup bucket
backups.description: The deploy action creates a Cloudflare R2 bucket per environment (edustack-sandbox-N-backups) and wires the S3-compatible endpoint into the backend. Database backups upload there automatically; if R2 credentials are missing the step is skipped and backups stay on the Fly volume.

domains.title: Custom domains
domains.description: Everything lives under is-edustack.org: sandbox-N.is-edustack.org → Cloudflare Pages (frontend), be-sandbox-N.is-edustack.org → Fly.io backend, mail-sandbox-N.is-edustack.org → MailDev. Backend + MailDev get TLS via Fly Let's Encrypt; the frontend is proxied through Cloudflare.

deployment.workflowDescription: Provisioning is driven by .github/workflows/deploy-env.yml. Trigger it from GitHub → Actions → Deploy Environment → Run workflow. Pick an env_id (e.g. 1) and one of the actions below. Every step — Fly apps, R2 bucket, DNS records, Pages project — is idempotent, so you can safely re-run on the same env_id.

deploy.title: deploy
deploy.description: Full provision: create the backend Fly app + 1 GB volume, the MailDev Fly app, the R2 backup bucket, stage secrets, flyctl deploy both apps, attach DNS + TLS, build and push the frontend to Cloudflare Pages. Use for the first run and every subsequent push.

delete.title: delete
delete.description: Destroys both Fly apps (backend machines + volume and MailDev), empties and removes the R2 backup bucket, deletes all three CNAMEs, and removes the Cloudflare Pages project. Use when tearing an environment down.

deployment.secretsDescription: Add these under Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret. The required ones must be present before the first run; the recommended ones get sensible random fallbacks but you should always set them in production. If the R2 keys are missing the deploy step skips bucket provisioning and backups stay on the Fly volume.

secrets.fly-api-token: Fly.io API token (account-wide so it can create apps). flyctl auth login, then flyctl auth token.
secrets.cloudflare-api-token: API token for Pages deploys, DNS edits, and R2 bucket management. Cloudflare → My Profile → API Tokens → Create Token with: Account → Cloudflare Pages: Edit, Account → Workers R2 Storage: Edit, Account → Account Settings: Read, Zone → Zone: Read (is-edustack.org), Zone → DNS: Edit (is-edustack.org), User → User Details: Read.
secrets.cloudflare-account-id: Cloudflare account ID. Found in the right sidebar of any Workers / domain overview in the dashboard.
secrets.jwt-secret: Random string (≥ 32 chars) used to sign JWT cookies. Pushed into Fly via flyctl secrets set on every deploy.
secrets.encryption-key: AES-256 key — exactly 32 hex characters. Encrypts provider API keys in the SystemSecret table. Losing it makes those rows unreadable.
secrets.mcp-auth-token: Shared bearer token between the backend and MCP server. Generated with openssl rand -hex 32.
secrets.r2-access-key-id: S3-compatible access key ID for the R2 backup bucket. Cloudflare → R2 → Manage R2 API Tokens → Create API token (Object Read & Write). Separate from CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN.
secrets.r2-secret-access-key: S3-compatible secret access key, returned alongside R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID when you create the R2 API token. Cloudflare shows it once — store it immediately.

prereqs.cloudflare: Add the zone is-edustack.org (Websites → Add a site) and point your registrar's nameservers to Cloudflare.
prereqs.fly: Create a Fly.io account (flyctl auth signup) and confirm your org slug is personal (default for personal accounts). Override FLY_ORG in deploy-env.yml if you use a custom org.

deployment.costs: Each environment is one Fly.io machine (shared-cpu-1x, 512 MB) that auto-stops when idle, plus a 1 GB volume (~$0.15/month) that accrues even while stopped. Fly.io offers a 7-day trial; afterwards a payment method is required. To drop to zero between demos, run the delete action and deploy again later. Realistic cost at demo / low-traffic usage is roughly $1–2 per environment per month. Cloudflare Pages stays on the free tier comfortably.
deployment.readmeLink: Full step-by-step in the repository README

backendStatus.title: Backend Status & Health
backendStatus.description: Every backend exposes a public status page, a JSON health endpoint that gates the frontend boot, and full Swagger / OpenAPI documentation. The paths below are relative — prepend them with the backend's base URL for the environment you want to inspect.
backendStatus.baseUrlHint: Swap sandbox-1 for any other env_id, e.g. be-sandbox-2.is-edustack.org.
backendStatus.statusDescription: The backend root path renders a lightweight status page that confirms the API is online, names the environment, surfaces the database connection state and Node.js runtime, and links straight into the OpenAPI docs. Use it as a quick "is it up?" check before opening the frontend.
backendStatus.healthDescription: The frontend polls GET /api/health on every load and keeps showing the EduStack IS loading screen until the response is healthy. If the backend is cold-starting, mid-migration, or unreachable, the loader stays visible — users never land on a half-booted app. The endpoint also reports uptime, memory, version, commit, and build time so monitoring can scrape it.
backendStatus.docsDescription: Every backend ships with interactive Swagger UI covering all modules — auth, init, students, teachers, schools, and the rest. Endpoints are grouped by module, request and response schemas are inferred from DTOs, and you can authorize once via JWT to call live endpoints straight from the browser.

setup.title: System Setup
setup.description: Initialization runs once per environment. Two GitHub Actions deploy toggles decide whether the system bootstraps itself with demo data and whether the login screen exposes a developer helper — the manual System Setup screen only appears when auto-seeding is disabled.

workflowToggles.title: Deploy workflow toggles
workflowToggles.description: Two checkboxes on the Deploy Environment workflow change how the first boot of a sandbox behaves. Both default to ON so a freshly created env_id deploys ready for demos. They have no effect on environments that have already been initialized — re-running the workflow with different values does not rewind the database.
workflowToggles.helperTitle: Enable login helper (demo-user picker on login screen) — deploy only
workflowToggles.helperDescription: Renders a developer-only picker on the login screen with one-click sign-in for every user.
workflowToggles.seedTitle: Auto-seed demo data on first boot — deploy only
workflowToggles.seedDescription: Skips the manual System Setup screen and provisions schools, users, schedules, and the System Administrator automatically.

autoSeed.title: Auto-seeded demo data
autoSeed.description: When auto-seed is on, the backend bypasses the System Setup screen entirely on first boot and inserts the full demo dataset. A System Administrator account is created automatically and used to bring the rest of the schools online.
autoSeed.securityNote: Every account generated by the seeder shares the same Demo1234! password. Rotate it (or disable the accounts you don't need) before exposing the environment to real users.

loginHelper.title: Login helper
loginHelper.description: While the login helper toggle is on, the login screen renders a picker of every user in the system; clicking a card signs in immediately, no password needed. The shortcut deactivates per-user the moment that user changes their password from their profile — newly hardened accounts fall back to the standard email + password form, even while the helper stays available for everyone else.

setup.manualDescription: When auto-seed is off, the first request to the frontend lands on the System Setup screen. Pick between a fresh install (create the first System Administrator now) or restoring an existing .sqlite backup.

option1.title: Fresh install
option1.description: Create the first System Administrator through the in-app form: name, surname, admin email, password, and confirmation. After submission the system goes live with an empty database — schools and users are then added through the System Administrator console.

option2.title: Restore from backup
option2.description: Pick the "Restore from backup" tile and upload a previously exported .sqlite file. The current database is replaced with the contents of the backup; the upload button stays disabled until a file is selected.

login.title: User Authentication
login.description: The login screen renders four variants — login helper on/off crossed with SSO available or not — plus a password reset flow. Which variant a user sees depends on the deploy-time login helper toggle and on whether the System Administrator has registered any SSO providers.

helper.title: Login helper
helper.description: Developer-only picker on the login screen for one-click sign-in as any user. Controlled by the auto-deploy toggle and self-deactivates per user once that user changes their password from their profile.

credentials.title: Email & password
credentials.description: Standard secure authentication using hashed passwords (Argon2) stored in the SQLite database. Always available — even when the helper is on, users can switch to the regular form.

sso.title: SSO integration
sso.description: Enterprise-grade login via Google, GitHub, and Microsoft. SSO buttons only appear when the System Administrator has configured at least one provider; otherwise the screen shows email + password only.

reset.title: Password reset
reset.description: Three-step forgot-password flow accessible from the login screen. (1) The user requests a reset by entering their email. (2) A tokenized reset link is delivered via the configured SMTP server — MailDev in sandbox environments. (3) Following the link opens a Set New Password form with a new + confirm field. The token is valid for the duration configured in System security.

aiTutor.title: AI Tutor
aiTutor.description: Conversational AI assistant embedded in every role. The tutor is grounded by the system prompts (System Administrator → System Prompts) so it stays scoped to school content. Pick a provider/model (Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, OpenCode.ai — whichever the System Administrator has configured), ask a question, and the answer streams back in the same window. Available to students, parents, teachers, deputies, headmasters, and system admins.

roles.title: User Roles
roles.intro: Below is the per-role walkthrough. Permissions overlap heavily in code, but each role uses the system in a different way. The cards below summarize what each role actually does day-to-day.

schoolManagement.title: Headmaster & Deputy
schoolManagement.body: Primary school management — academic year setup, classes and subjects, schedule planner, curriculum, substitutions, bulk user invites. They also have access to everything Teacher does.

teacher.title: Teacher
teacher.body: Active work in the classroom — running lessons, grading (numeric + verbal with AI polish), attendance, class book, lesson preparations, and direct messaging.

student.title: Student
student.body: View-only consumer — sees their own grades, schedule, materials, attendance, community posts, and chats with the AI Tutor. Nothing they submit changes school state.

parent.title: Parent
parent.body: Mostly view-only like the student (sees the child's grades, schedule, messages) plus one active responsibility: submitting absence excuses for the teacher to approve.

systemAdmin.title: System Administrator
systemAdmin.description: The ultimate orchestrator. The sidebar exposes five top-level destinations — Dashboard, Schools, Users, System Settings, and System Prompts — and every administrative action on the platform lives in one of them.
systemAdmin.functions: Multi-tenant school lifecycle, system-admin promotion, AI provider keys and usage analytics, SSO identity providers, security hardening, audit log, database backups, AI-driven test data generation, and a read-only catalogue of every prompt the system sends to AI providers.

schools.title: School management
schools.description: The Schools panel is the multi-tenant control center. Each row shows a school with headmaster and deputy badges; the row actions cover entering the tenant as any role (system admin, headmaster, or deputy) for support work, editing metadata and the SSO email-match policy, and a type-to-confirm delete that wipes every record tied to the tenant. Creating a school is a single modal that either provisions a brand-new headmaster on the spot or links an existing user.

users.title: System administrators
users.description: Lists every user with global system privileges and lets you promote new ones. Adding an admin checks the email — if the user already exists, they are promoted in place; otherwise the form expects first and last name and a fresh account is provisioned. Removal deactivates the account and revokes admin rights.

settings.title: System settings
settings.description: All global configuration lives here under six tabs: AI management, SSO integration, System (security), Monitoring, Backups, and Test data. Each tab is described below.

ai.title: AI management
ai.description: Single screen for AI provider keys (Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, OpenCode.ai) plus current-month usage analytics — total tokens, input/output split, request count, top schools, per-provider breakdown, and daily usage chart. Each provider row links straight to its developer portal so an admin can obtain the key. Keys are encrypted at rest (AES-256) and removed via a confirmation dialog.

sso.title: SSO integration
sso.description: Configure OAuth identity providers (Google, GitHub, Microsoft, Apple). The page shows the exact callback URL the backend expects ('{backendUrl}'/api/auth/callback/[provider]) plus direct links to each provider's developer portal. The edit modal stores Client ID and Client Secret (encrypted), exposes an enable/disable toggle, and lets you delete the configuration entirely.

security.title: System security
security.description: Three knobs control login hardening: maximum failed attempts before lockout, lockout duration in minutes, and reset-token validity in minutes.

monitoring.title: Monitoring & audit log
monitoring.description: Live backend status (uptime, database, memory, version, commit, build time) sits above the system audit log. The audit table records every high-impact action — admin promotion, logins, entity edits — and supports filtering by date range, action type, entity, and user id.

backups.title: Database backups
backups.description: Lists every backup of the Cloudflare D1 (SQLite) database stored in the per-environment R2 bucket. Each row exposes download, restore, and delete actions. Manual backups are named via the create modal; restoring replaces the live database with the chosen snapshot.

testData.title: Test data generator
testData.description: Generates a complete school with realistic test data: pick the school type (1st stage, 1st & 2nd stage, 8-year gymnázium, or 4-year gymnázium), set teacher / student / parent counts split between active and invited, and toggle which modules to seed (subjects, schedule, classification, messages, attendance, report cards, community). The AI button generates a realistic Czech school name. Below sits a Danger zone — wipe a single school's data or the entire system.

prompts.title: System prompts
prompts.description: Read-only catalogue of every prompt the backend uses to talk to AI models — chat base instruction, chat for system admins, school-name generator, text refinement, thematic plan generator, student recommendations, class performance analysis, and the rest. Each entry shows the identifier, owning service, short description, and full template so admins know exactly what gets sent to providers.

schoolStaff.title: Headmaster & Deputy
schoolStaff.description: Headmaster and Deputy share the same backend permissions and use the same shared classroom toolkit as Teacher (Schedule, Grading, Attendance, Class Book, Messages, Community). Their primary job, though, is running the school — academic year setup, classes and subjects, the schedule planner, curriculum, substitutions — covered in the School Management chapter. The pages below document the shared toolkit; teacher-specific walkthroughs live in the Teacher chapter that follows. Note: 'School Administrator' is not a real role — it's a label the System Administrator can pick when entering a tenant via 'Vstoupit jako…', with the same permissions as Headmaster or Deputy.
schoolStaff.functions: Same toolkit as Teacher (schedule, grading, attendance, class book, messages, community) plus the full School Management section (academic year setup, users, classrooms, ŠVP, schedule planner, substitutions, report cards, events).

dashboard.title: Role colors — instant identification
dashboard.description: Every school-staff role gets its own accent color so you always know which role the current session is in (especially useful when a System Administrator hops between roles via 'Vstoupit jako'). The dashboard layout itself is identical for all three — unread messages, personal task list, upcoming events. The differences are: Headmaster — blue (and the 'School Administrator' override inherits the same blue, since it shares Headmaster's permissions). Deputy — a lighter sky. Teacher — red. The three screenshots below show the exact same dashboard side by side, with the only difference being the color of the sidebar accents, badges, and primary buttons.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.description: View-only timetable. Switch tabs to see your own schedule, an entire class, a specific teacher, or a classroom — all filterable by academic year and semester (or 'All' to see both halves).

grading.title: Grading
grading.description: Per-class grade book filterable by academic year, class, and semester. The empty state shows every student × subject cell with a quick '+' to add a grade. The New Grade modal accepts a numeric mark (1–5 with optional weight) plus a written verbal assessment; 'Polish with AI' generates three drafts — formal, encouraging, concise — that the user can accept or regenerate with extra instructions. Clicking a student's name opens their per-subject overview with all grades, weights, dates, and the saved verbal assessments.

images.newFilled: New grade — filled with verbal text

attendance.title: Attendance
attendance.description: Record presence per class and date, see per-student statistics, manage parent excuses (approve / reject), and review escalations for chronic absences. The Excuses tab lists pending requests with the parent, dates, and reason; the row turns green when approved or red when rejected.

classBook.title: Class book
classBook.description: Electronic class book wired to the schedule. Pick a class and date to see what was taught each lesson, sign entries, and print the day's records for the chosen range of dates.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: 1:1 messaging accessible to every role (student, parent, teacher, deputy, headmaster). Search recipients by name or email and start a conversation. Headmaster and Deputy additionally see a 'Hromadná zpráva' (broadcast) button that delivers a single message to an entire class or the whole school.

community.title: Community
community.description: School-wide bulletin, polls, and events. Anyone can read; teachers, deputies, and headmasters can create posts. Polls support single- or multi-choice voting with live percentages. Events show YES / MAYBE / NO RSVP counts and can be created with title, description, time range, and location — the new event then appears inline in the events list.

images.eventsAfterCreate: Events list after creating a new event

teacher.title: Teacher
teacher.description: Active classroom worker — uses the same shared toolkit as Headmaster and Deputy (Schedule, Grading, Attendance, Class Book, Messages, Community — see the previous chapter) but focuses on active subject and grade work rather than school-wide configuration. Teachers see a leaner sidebar without the School Management section, and their accent color is red so any active session is instantly recognisable.
teacher.functions: Active classroom work: leading subjects, recording grades with AI-polished verbal assessments, signing class-book entries, tracking attendance, communicating with students and parents, contributing to the community feed.

tour.title: Teacher walkthrough
tour.description: Sequential walkthrough of the teacher view from sign-in through every common daily action. Teachers see a leaner sidebar than Headmaster / Deputy — no 'Správa školy' submenu — but everything they need for running classes is one click away.

schoolManagement.title: School management
schoolManagement.description: Additional toolkit available only to Headmaster and Deputy (and, by extension, a System Administrator using 'Vstoupit jako'). Covers the school-wide configuration that sits behind the daily classroom work — academic year setup, classes and subjects, schedule planner, curriculum, substitutions, and bulk user invitations.
schoolManagement.functions: Academic year & semester rollover, class and subject management, curriculum versioning (SVP/RVP mapping), schedule planner with collision detection, substitutions, bulk user invitations.

yearSetup.title: Academic year setup
yearSetup.description: Roll the school forward: open a new academic year, define its semesters, and tick which modules should be live for the year.

users.title: Users
users.description: Local user directory. Invite teachers, students, and parents one at a time or in bulk via CSV import; open a user's detail to edit profile information, assign classes, and change roles.

classrooms.title: Buildings & classrooms
classrooms.description: Define the physical layout of the school — buildings, floors, and individual classrooms. Each classroom can be edited with capacity, equipment, and the floor it sits on.

bellSchedule.title: Bell schedule
bellSchedule.description: Configure lesson start and end times for the school day. Used by every timetable and class-book view.

rvp.title: Framework curriculum (RVP)
rvp.description: Browse the national Framework Education Programme (RVP) — domains, competencies, and outputs. Used as the upstream source when building the school's ŠVP and thematic plans.

svp.title: School curriculum (ŠVP)
svp.description: Define the school's own curriculum on top of RVP: which subjects each grade has, with what weekly hour allocation, mapped to RVP competencies. The ŠVP is versioned — you can publish a new version for the upcoming year while keeping the active one untouched.

whiteBook.title: White Book
whiteBook.description: The ministerial White Book — the strategic document underpinning the Czech educational policy. Surfaced here as reading material for staff designing the ŠVP and thematic plans.

schedule.title: Schedule planner
schedule.description: Build the school's weekly timetable. Drag subjects onto class / teacher / room slots; the planner flags collisions in real time. The result feeds the read-only Schedule view that every role uses day-to-day.

substitutions.title: Substitutions
substitutions.description: Manage daily absences and reassignments. Pick the affected lessons and assign a substitute teacher, change the room, or swap the subject — affected users see the change in their personal schedule immediately.

scheduleComparison.title: Schedule comparison
scheduleComparison.description: Diff two schedule versions side-by-side. Useful when rolling a semester or applying bulk changes — see exactly what moved before publishing the new version.

thematicPlans.title: Thematic plans
thematicPlans.description: Per-subject teaching plan for the year. Define topics, hours, and which RVP outputs they cover. Teachers reference the plan from their lesson-prep screen; the progress chart shows how far the actual taught material has advanced against the plan.

lessonPrep.title: Lesson preparations
lessonPrep.description: Per-lesson teaching notes. A preparation links to a thematic-plan topic and is reused when signing the class book. Optional AI assistant generates a draft preparation from the topic and grade level.

materials.title: Teaching materials
materials.description: Shared library of files attached to subjects, thematic plans, or individual preparations. Visible to teachers of the subject; can be exposed to students from a lesson preparation.

reportCards.title: Report cards (vysvědčení)
reportCards.description: End-of-semester deliverable. Pick the template, generate report cards for the chosen class, track per-student progress, and export the final PDF batch.

clubs.title: Clubs
clubs.description: After-school activities — name, schedule, capacity, leading teacher, and the list of enrolled students. Each club has its own attendance and announcements stream.

events.title: School events
events.description: School-wide calendar (trips, parent meetings, holidays). Create with title, date range, location, and audience; the event then surfaces in the community Events tab for the affected users.

disciplinary.title: Disciplinary measures
disciplinary.description: Record commendations, reprimands, and formal disciplinary measures issued to students. Each entry is attached to a student's profile and shows up in their parent's view.

audit.title: School audit log
audit.description: School-scoped audit log — every action that mutates school data is recorded with actor, timestamp, and entity. Mirrors the system-wide audit log but restricted to this tenant.

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: Mostly mirrors the Student view of their child — grades, schedule, classification details, community announcements, AI Tutor — but adds one active responsibility: submitting parental excuses for absences. The Teacher / Deputy / Headmaster then approves or rejects each excuse from the Attendance tab.
parent.functions: View child's grades and progress, view personal schedule and substitutions, submit and track parental excuses for absences, message teachers and school management, participate in community polls and event RSVPs.

tour.title: Parent walkthrough
tour.description: Sequential walkthrough of the parent view — from sign-in to the child's grades and schedule, communication with teachers, and the absence-excuse flow. The accent color is green so parents instantly recognize their role.

student.title: Student
student.description: View-only consumer of the system. Sees own grades and per-subject classification, personal schedule with substitutions, attendance and disciplinary records, teaching materials, community posts and event RSVPs, and chats with the AI Tutor. Nothing the student submits changes school-side state — even the AI Tutor conversation is local to the student.
student.functions: Personal dashboard with grade overview, interactive daily and weekly schedule, community messaging and polls, access to teaching materials, AI Tutor for educational support.

tour.title: Student walkthrough
tour.description: Sequential walkthrough of the student view — from sign-in to grades, schedule, messages, and community. The accent color is orange so students immediately recognize their role.

Footer.description: Modern information system designed to transform informatics education through interactive simulation.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: At Edu Stack IS, we take your privacy seriously. This presentation website is purely informational.

section1.title: Data Collection
section1.content: We do not collect any personal data from visitors of this presentation website. No cookies are used for tracking or profiling.

section2.title: Information System Data
section2.content: The Edu Stack IS application itself handles data according to the configurations set by the hosting educational institution. For the sandbox version, data is handled according to our non-commercial open-source rules.

section3.title: Third-Party Services
section3.content: This site is hosted on Cloudflare Pages. Cloudflare may collect standard web server logs for security and performance purposes.

Terms.title: Terms of Service
Terms.intro: By accessing this website, you agree to the following terms.

section1.title: Usage of Content
section1.content: The content on this website is for educational and presentation purposes. You may use the information here to understand the Edu Stack IS project.

section2.title: Software License
section2.content: The Edu Stack IS software is open-source. Usage is restricted to non-commercial educational purposes. Any commercial usage requires a written agreement with the author.

section3.title: Disclaimer
section3.content: The information provided here is 'as is' without any warranties. We are not responsible for any issues arising from the use of the information or software presented here.
